title: Meta AI business assistant now available in Australia
full_text: F.Y.I. Meta AI business assistant now available in Australia April 23, 2026 9:20 Meta’s AI business assistant is now available to advertisers in Australia as the technology rolls out beyond the US market The announcement: Today, Meta announced the global beta expansion of Meta AI business assistant to advertisers of all sizes across major global markets, with local language support now available across APAC and ANZ as well as US, EMEA and LATAM regions. Since launching with small businesses in the US in October 2025, advertisers have told us it helps them work faster and make more confident decisions. Specifically, businesses resolved common account issues at a 20% higher rate with Meta AI business assistant — from account optimisation to core management tasks. And small business advertisers saw a 12% decrease in cost per result after applying opportunity score recommendations in Meta AI business assistant. For Australian advertisers and businesses now gaining access, Meta AI business assistant can help: Optimise campaign performance – Get tailored recommendations powered by your business data, instantly analyse campaign results, and benchmark performance to surface new opportunities. Get answers on the go – Ask questions about your account or campaigns in plain language and receive real-time, personalised guidance. Resolve account issues in seconds – Restore disabled accounts, update spend limits, and troubleshoot payment or delivery errors — without waiting on support queues. For more information, read the full announcement: https://www.facebook.com/business/news/meta-ai-business-assistant-expands-globally-with-more-languages?utm_source=mighty_mumbrella Statement Will Easton, Managing Director, Meta Australia and New Zealand “We know that Australian and New Zealand businesses, whether they’re a local start-up or a leading agency, want smarter tools that save time and drive real results. Meta AI business assistant does exactly that. Built directly into the ad tools our clients already use, it delivers personalised recommendations, instant account support, and performance insights — all powered by AI. This announcement builds on our roadmap of innovative products and brings to life Meta’s AI vision to give every business or agency powerful AI tools, enabling them to do more with less and thrive in any environment.” Source: Meta press release TOPICS META Have your say Login with: Continue with LinkedIn Or comment anonymously Your comment will be marked as unverified Name Comment ADVERTISEMENT LATEST What Australia's coming housing crisis means for marketers Rohan Lund announced as new CEO of Southern Cross Austereo Vinyl eats into cash reserves and pushes back profitability date Rose Herceg promoted as WPP revives ANZ CEO role Snap axes APAC comms chief Natasha Brack amid AI-driven restructure
summary: Meta has launched its AI business assistant in Australia, expanding its availability to advertisers across major global markets. The assistant aims to enhance campaign performance, provide real-time guidance, and resolve account issues quickly. Early users in the US reported improved efficiency and reduced costs, indicating potential benefits for Australian businesses as well.
